title: Task-5 Editing and Deleting Own Comments

content.problemStatement:
Now we will add the functionality that will allow users to edit or delete their own comments, but only within a 5-minute window after posting. Deleting a comment should not remove its replies.

**After this task:**
- **"Edit"** and **"Delete"** buttons will appear on a user's own comments.
- The **"Delete"** button will immediately "soft delete" the comment. The content will be replaced with a message like "[This comment was deleted]", but the comment's container and any replies beneath it will remain.
- The **"Edit"** button will lead to a new page with a form to update the comment's content.
- This **"Edit"* functionality will only work if the comment was created less than 5 minutes ago. 
### Files to Create/Modify for this Task:
- comment_system/settings.py **(Done for you)** *(line #125)*
   - Add a new setting at the end of the file to control the edit timeout. This makes it easy to change later.
- comments/models.py 
   - Add the `is_deleted` field to the Comment model. 
   - More importantly, add methods (`can_be_edited_by`, `can_be_deleted_by`) that contain the permission logic. 
   - This keeps the business logic in the model, where it belongs.
- comments/views.py 
   - Add the new `edit_comment` and `delete_comment` views. 
   - They both start by checking for permissions using the new methods on the Comment model.
- comments/urls.py 
   - Add the new URL patterns for the edit and delete views.
- comments/templates/comments/edit_comment.html **(Done for you)**
   - Created this new template. It's a simple page that displays the form for editing a comment.
- comments/templates/comments/comment_display.html **(Done for you)**
   - Updated this template to:
     -  Add deleted to the CSS class list if the comment is_deleted.
     - Show a "[This comment was deleted]" message instead of the content.
     - Hide all actions (vote, reply, etc.) for deleted comments.
     - Show "Edit" and "Delete" buttons only if the logged-in user is the comment's author.

**After completing the above:**
- Click on **RUN** and open the browser on a new tab.
- Log in and post a new comment. You should see **"Edit"** and **"Delete"** buttons on it.
- Click the "Delete" button. The page should reload, and the comment's text should change, but it should still be visible.
- Post another comment and immediately click "Edit". It should take you to the edit page. Save a change and verify it appears.
- Wait more than 5 minutes for that same comment, then try to access its edit URL directly (or click the button if it's still there). The server should now show an error message and deny access.
